methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $4.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Nuclear Energy. Range: $1.0B - $10.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Kairos Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Late Stage), post-money $1.6B+

data.narrative:
TerraPower is a late-stage nuclear energy company founded by Bill Gates, currently valued at $4.0B post-money following $1.7B+ in cumulative capital raised. The peer universe comprises 10 comparable companies across nuclear energy: 3 public (Oklo, Nano Nuclear Energy, and one other) and 7 private firms, with valuation range spanning $1.0B to $10.0B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $4.0B last post-money valuation and applying a 1.2x multiplier typical of late-stage progression, yields $4.8B. Peer-median analysis across 5 same-sector comparables—Oklo ($10.0B public), X-Energy ($2.0B IPO), Kairos Power ($1.6B late stage), Nano Nuclear Energy ($1.5B public), and Zap Energy ($1.0B Series D)—produces a median of $1.6B, though the range is wide at $1.0B–$10.0B.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.4B (low) to $5.5B (high), with a median of $3.2B. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around which peer cohort is most relevant: whether TerraPower should anchor to earlier-stage private companies or to recently public nuclear peers trading at premium multiples. The divergence between stage-progression ($4.8B) and peer-median ($1.6B) methods suggests sensitivity to assumptions about comparable company selection and the sustainability of current public-market valuations in the nuclear sector.
